title: The Pool Guy

jokes.text: When did tick-tack-toe become the pound sign? Why not call it what it is? 'Leave your numeric message now, and then press Archie's head.'
jokes.setup: Jerry questions when the tic-tac-toe symbol became the pound sign
jokes.punchline: Suggests calling it 'Archie's head' referencing the comic character's hairstyle
jokes.explanation: Classic Jerry observational humor with a creative visual metaphor. The Archie reference is unexpected and clever.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: When did the tic-tac-toe symbol become the pound sign? Nobody decided that. It just happened. We should call it 'Archie's head.' That's what it looks like—Archie's hair.

jokes.text: This is like the harassment key for people in a fight, you know.
jokes.setup: Jerry analyzing the redial button function
jokes.punchline: Calls it the 'harassment key' for fighting couples
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor that rings true about technology and relationships.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: The redial button is just the harassment key. That's all it is. For fighting couples. You hang up on someone, they hang up on you, boom — redial. It's like a technological way to say 'we're not done arguing yet.'

jokes.text: Yeah, me too.
jokes.setup: Jerry asks who would win in a fight between him and George
jokes.punchline: Both think they would obviously win
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character work - shows both their delusions of physical prowess.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Who do you think would win in a fight between you and me?

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: Oh, I'd win. Easily.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: You? You'd win?

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: I'd destroy you.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: I don't think so.

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: Oh please, I'd take you apart.

jokes.text: Mano a baldo.
jokes.setup: Elaine asks if they mean a real fight
jokes.punchline: Jerry twists 'mano a mano' to reference George's baldness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that's both witty and character-specific. Highly quotable and memorable.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Yeah, it's a real fight. Mano a mano. Well, in George's case, it's more like mano a... no mano.

jokes.text: George fights dirty. Pull hair, poke eyes, groin stuff. Whatever I gotta do.
jokes.setup: Elaine explains why George would win
jokes.punchline: George proudly confirms he'd fight dirty without shame
jokes.explanation: Perfect George characterization - his shameless admission to fighting dirty is very on-brand.

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: You'd fight dirty, you wouldn't care about winning fair and square.

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: That's right! I would fight dirty! I have no shame!

jokes.text: Would I want to see what Mary Todd wore to Lincoln's funeral?
jokes.setup: Elaine invites Jerry to historical clothing exhibit
jokes.punchline: Jerry's dismissive question about the exhibit's appeal
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jerry's disinterest in culture, delivered with perfect deadpan tone.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: What's the big deal? It's just old clothes. I mean, how interesting can fabric from 200 years ago be?

jokes.text: You're a man's woman. You hate other women and they hate you.
jokes.setup: Elaine complains about having no female friends
jokes.punchline: Jerry's blunt analysis of why she has no female friends
jokes.explanation: Sharp character observation delivered with Jerry's trademark bluntness. Rings true about Elaine.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Well, you're not very nice.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: You're like a man.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: You act like a man, you dress like a man, you think like a man.

jokes.text: Saddle up and ride.
jokes.setup: Making movie plans
jokes.punchline: Jerry's over-the-top cowboy response to simple movie time
jokes.explanation: Mildly amusing absurdist response but not particularly memorable or clever.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: What time do you want to see the movie?

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: High noon.

jokes.text: There's nothing more pathetic than a grown man who's afraid of a woman.
jokes.setup: George hesitates to call Susan
jokes.punchline: Jerry's mock-serious pronouncement while George is clearly afraid
jokes.explanation: Good setup for George's cowardice but not a standout line.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: You have to call her. You can't just not call her.

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: I know, I know. I'm going to call her.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: So call her.

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: I'll call her. I just... I need a moment.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: A man who cannot make a phone call is not a man.

jokes.text: This world here, this is George's sanctuary. If Susan comes into contact with this world, his worlds collide.
jokes.setup: Jerry doesn't see the problem with Elaine befriending Susan
jokes.punchline: Kramer introduces the 'worlds colliding' theory
jokes.explanation: Introduction of a classic Seinfeld concept that became culturally referenced. Great setup for the episode's main theme.

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: These are two separate worlds, Jerry. You don't want these two worlds to collide!

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Why would they collide?

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: Well, I don't know, but I do know that if Elaine and Susan meet, the world as we know it will cease to exist.

jokes.text: Chicks, man. Too many chicks know my number.
jokes.setup: Ramon explains why he's getting a new phone number
jokes.punchline: His exaggerated claim about too many women
jokes.explanation: Fairly generic 'player' character joke that relies on the obviousness of his delusion.

dialogue.speaker: Ramon
dialogue.line: I'm getting a new phone number.

dialogue.speaker: Ramon
dialogue.line: I've got too many women calling me.

jokes.text: Hey, well, stay out of the deep end.
jokes.setup: Ramon says he was fired for putting too much chlorine in pool
jokes.punchline: Jerry's dry pun about avoiding the dangerous area
jokes.explanation: Quick, dry wordplay that works but isn't remarkable. Good economy.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Well, I guess you could say you really... chlorinated your career opportunities.

jokes.text: What's in the deep end?
jokes.setup: Jerry's warning about the deep end
jokes.punchline: George's oblivious question reveals he missed the implication
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing George's obliviousness to the danger implication.

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: Wait, what deep end? I thought we were talking about the pool!

jokes.text: I really think I should have been consulted about this.
jokes.setup: Elaine wants to ask Susan to lunch
jokes.punchline: George's controlling demand to be consulted about his girlfriend's friendships
jokes.explanation: Perfect George neurosis about control and boundaries in relationships.

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: I want to ask Susan to lunch.

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa. You can't just ask Susan to lunch without consulting me first. She's my girlfriend. I need to approve all of her friendships.

jokes.text: A little buffer zone.
jokes.setup: Jerry suggests they don't sit next to Ramon at movies
jokes.punchline: His clinical term for avoiding unwanted social contact
jokes.explanation: Good Jerry-esque term for a universal social situation. Economical and relatable.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: We should have a clinical term for avoiding unwanted social contact. Like when you don't want to sit next to someone at the movies.

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: What would we call it?

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: The Big Snub.

jokes.text: Well, these seats have no lumbar.
jokes.setup: George keeps moving seats awkwardly
jokes.punchline: His pretentious excuse about lumbar support
jokes.explanation: Classic George pretentious excuse for his neurotic behavior.

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: What? I'm just adjusting for lumbar support. My back needs proper orthopedic alignment.

jokes.text: So, well, then I had to kill him and the police are still looking for me.
jokes.setup: Jerry needs to pretend their conversation is interesting
jokes.punchline: His absurdly dramatic fake conversation topic
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist escalation for what would make conversation seem 'interesting.'

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: So I was thinking about the button on my shirt... and how it's just sitting there. Doing nothing. And I thought, 'What if that button could talk?' And it would say, 'Hey, I've been holding this fabric together for three years now, and nobody's even acknowledged me. I'm like the unsung hero of your wardrobe.' And then I realized, buttons don't talk. But if they did, that button would have some serious issues to work through.

jokes.text: Worlds collide. Yes, it blows up.
jokes.setup: Jerry starts explaining the worlds theory
jokes.punchline: George's dramatic confirmation with explosive imagery
jokes.explanation: Good callback with escalation. George's dramatic language adds to the absurdity.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: So I have this theory about the world.

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: Oh yeah? What is it?

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Well, I think everything is just falling apart. Slowly at first, then all at once.

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: That's it? That's your theory?

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Yeah.

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: The world's not falling apart, Jerry. The world's exploding. We're all just standing here while everything around us is blowing up in our faces!

jokes.text: It's just common sense. Anybody knows you gotta keep your worlds apart.
jokes.setup: Jerry didn't know about worlds theory
jokes.punchline: George treats this neurotic concept as universal wisdom
jokes.explanation: Perfect George moment - treating his neurosis as obvious universal truth.

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: Jerry, there are two worlds. There's the real world, and there's the world inside your head.

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: You gotta learn to live in both worlds. That's what I do.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: You live in two worlds?

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: Yeah. This world, and the world in here. And in here, I'm married, I'm a millionaire, I'm an astronaut.

jokes.text: Well, wait a second. Don't you see? That's 555-FlLK. But 555-FlLM is Moviefone.
jokes.setup: Kramer's new number causes wrong number calls
jokes.punchline: Jerry realizes it's one letter off from Moviefone
jokes.explanation: Clever setup for the entire Moviefone plotline. Good observational detective work by Jerry.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Wait, what's your number?

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: 555-FILK

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: 555-FILK? That's one letter off from Moviefone!

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: You're gonna get all their calls.

jokes.text: So I'm filk. You're filk.
jokes.setup: Understanding the phone number mix-up
jokes.punchline: The nonsensical word 'filk' as Kramer's phone identity
jokes.explanation: Amusing wordplay that sets up Kramer's accidental Moviefone role.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: So what is this number people have been calling?

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: It's 'Filk'. That's my name now. Filk.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Filk? What kind of name is Filk?

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: It's a good name! It's got a nice ring to it.

jokes.text: Quite the décolletage for a suffragette.
jokes.setup: Discussing Susan B. Anthony's dress at museum
jokes.punchline: Susan's sophisticated observation about historical cleavage
jokes.explanation: Clever juxtaposition of sophisticated vocabulary with bawdy observation. Shows Susan's wit.

dialogue.speaker: Susan
dialogue.line: Look at the neckline on this dress. They really put the cleavage out there back then.

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: Well, what do you want? They didn't have push-up bras.

jokes.text: The vault?
jokes.setup: Elaine says she'll put information 'in the vault'
jokes.punchline: Susan's confused reaction to this phrase
jokes.explanation: Sets up the 'vault' concept that becomes important later. Simple but effective.

dialogue.speaker: Susan
dialogue.line: What vault?

jokes.text: And then back to her place, strip down to bra and panties for a tickle fight?
jokes.setup: Elaine describes doing 'girlie stuff' with Susan
jokes.punchline: Jerry's ridiculous male fantasy of what women do together
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jerry's juvenile understanding of female friendships.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: So what kind of 'girlie stuff' are we talking about here?

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: You know, we just hung out. Talked about clothes, makeup, relationships...

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Relationships? What did you say about relationships?

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: I don't know, normal stuff.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Were you... in your underwear?

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: What? No!

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Did you at least do each other's hair?

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: We talked, Jerry. That's it.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: This is very disappointing.

jokes.text: That's what you think girls do, isn't it? Yes, I do.
jokes.setup: Elaine calls out Jerry's fantasy
jokes.punchline: Jerry's shameless admission
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jerry moment - caught in his juvenile thinking and admitting it without shame.

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: You're fantasizing about her?

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: What? She's attractive!

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: She's your cousin!

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: A distant cousin. And in my fantasy, she's not related to me at all.

jokes.text: Yeah, Cupid's Rifle. Eight-thirty, Sony Lincoln Square.
jokes.setup: Kramer accidentally answers Moviefone calls
jokes.punchline: He starts providing movie information like the real service
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of the Moviefone premise. Very Kramer to just go with it.

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: Hello?

dialogue.speaker: Caller
dialogue.line: Yeah, I'd like to know what time the movie starts.

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: What movie?

dialogue.speaker: Caller
dialogue.line: The one playing at the mall.

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: Thank you for calling Moviefone. If you know the name of the movie you'd like to see, press 1. To browse by genre, press 2. To hear our special offers, press 3.

jokes.text: Yeah, Firestorm's good. I saw it yesterday. My buddy Jerry, he's seen it twice. You wanna talk to him?
jokes.setup: Kramer providing movie information
jokes.punchline: He offers personal movie reviews and tries to pass the caller to Jerry
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kramer escalation - turning impersonal service into personal recommendations.

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: Yeah, yeah, I got some reviews for you. Casablanca - great picture. Here's looking at you, kid.

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: The Godfather - tremendous picture. I'm gonna make him an offer he can't refuse.

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: Citizen Kane - masterpiece. Rosebud, my friend.

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: Hold on, I'm gonna get Jerry. He knows about movies too.

jokes.text: Bye-bye, Jerry.
jokes.setup: Ramon arrives unexpectedly and Elaine was about to leave
jokes.punchline: Her awkward quick exit to avoid the uncomfortable situation
jokes.explanation: Good awkward moment showing how people flee uncomfortable social situations.

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: Oh! Ramon! Hi! I was just... I have to go. Right now. Bye!

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: Sorry, I'm late for... something. Anything. I'll call you!

jokes.text: Look. You're a nice guy, but I actually only have three friends. I really can't handle any more.
jokes.setup: Jerry trying to get rid of clingy Ramon
jokes.punchline: His blunt explanation about friendship capacity limits
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jerry moment - treating friendship like a limited resource with specific capacity. Very quotable and relatable.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Ramon, I gotta be honest with you. I can only maintain a certain number of friendships at any given time. I'm at capacity. You're gonna have to be discontinued.

dialogue.speaker: Ramon
dialogue.line: Discontinued?

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Yeah, like a product line. You're being phased out.

jokes.text: Did you just say 'vault'? You got that from Elaine?
jokes.setup: Susan uses Elaine's 'vault' expression
jokes.punchline: George immediately recognizes the linguistic contamination
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of George's paranoia about world collision. Shows his hypersensitivity to influence.

dialogue.speaker: Susan
dialogue.line: I'm like a vault.

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: You're a vault? Since when are you a vault?

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: Elaine's a vault. You're not a vault.

jokes.text: Worlds are colliding. George is getting upset!
jokes.setup: The vault incident confirms George's fears
jokes.punchline: George's dramatic third-person outburst
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with George referring to himself in third person, showing his complete breakdown.

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: Why does everyone always find out about me? I'm not a big enough person to have secrets!

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: George is getting upset!

jokes.text: You see, right now, I have relationship George, but there is also independent George.
jokes.setup: George explaining his identity crisis
jokes.punchline: His clinical breakdown of his different personality aspects
jokes.explanation: Excellent character analysis that many people relate to - the idea of having different selves in different contexts. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Movie George, coffee-shop George, liar George, bawdy George.
jokes.setup: Continuing the multiple George identities theme
jokes.punchline: The specific catalog of his different personas, especially 'liar George'
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation with specific George types. 'Liar George' is particularly funny because of his self-awareness.

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: I'm out there Jerry, and I'm lovin' every minute of it.

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: I got a liar George, I got a businessman George. If I'm gonna do this, I gotta have a liar George.

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: My name is George. I'm unemployed and I live with my parents.

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: But there's the liar George. He's very good. He tells these lies, and he's very good at it. Nobody's ever caught liar George.

jokes.text: I love that George. Me too. And he's dying, Jerry.
jokes.setup: Jerry's appreciation for independent George
jokes.punchline: George's dramatic declaration that this George is dying
jokes.explanation: Good emotional escalation with George treating his personality like a dying person.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: You know, I have to say, I think you're doing great. You're really becoming your own man.

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: I am, aren't I? I'm independent, I'm confident...

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Yeah, you really are.

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: This George is dying.

jokes.text: A George divided against itself cannot stand.
jokes.setup: George's identity crisis monologue
jokes.punchline: Paraphrasing Lincoln's 'house divided' quote about himself
jokes.explanation: Brilliant adaptation of famous historical quote to personal neurosis. Perfect economy and highly quotable. This is classic Seinfeld writing.

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: A house divided against itself cannot stand. I am a house divided against myself. I cannot stand.

jokes.text: Hello, and welcome to Moviefone. Brought to you by The New York Times and Hot 97.
jokes.setup: Kramer answering what he thinks is another wrong number
jokes.punchline: He's now fully impersonating the Moviefone service with sponsors
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Kramer has fully committed to the role, even adding fake sponsors.

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: Hello?

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: No, this is not a movie theater.

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: Well, you've reached Moviefone. For showtimes, press 1. For ratings, press 2. For sponsorship opportunities, press 3.

jokes.text: Kramer, is that you?
jokes.setup: Elaine calls Moviefone and gets Kramer's impression
jokes.punchline: Her recognition breaking through his act
jokes.explanation: Good payoff moment showing the impersonation isn't perfect when people know him.

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: Hello? Hi, I'm calling about showtimes for—wait, is this Kramer?

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: This is Moviefone. What movie would you like to see?

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: Kramer, stop it! I know it's you!

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: I don't know what you're talking about. Press 1 for action movies.

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: That voice! The way you said 'Moviefone'! It's you!

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: Ma'am, please select a genre or hang up.

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: KRAMER! I can hear you grinning through the phone!

jokes.text: What time does Chow Fun start? I don't know.
jokes.setup: Elaine asks for movie times
jokes.punchline: Kramer's honest admission he doesn't actually know
jokes.explanation: Shows the flaw in Kramer's system - he's helpful but not actually informed.

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: What time is the movie?

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: I don't know. I have no idea what time it is.

jokes.text: Hey, Jerry, look at all the towels they gave me. I really hit the jackpot.
jokes.setup: Gym towel shortage mentioned earlier
jokes.punchline: Kramer's excitement over basic towels while Ramon suffers
jokes.explanation: Good ironic contrast between Kramer's windfall and Ramon's suffering from the towel shortage.

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: These towels are incredible!

dialogue.speaker: Ramon
dialogue.line: They're just towels.

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: Just towels? Do you know what I've been using? A gym sock and a newspaper!

jokes.text: Come on. Aren't you gonna join us? You know, I'm supposed to meet... someone. I'll wait for him outside.
jokes.setup: The awkward four-person lunch meeting
jokes.punchline: Jerry's obvious lie and escape attempt
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Jerry's desperation to avoid the collision of worlds.

jokes.text: Hey, hey, Georgie boy, over here.
jokes.setup: The uncomfortable lunch setup
jokes.punchline: Ramon's overly familiar greeting to George
jokes.explanation: Creates awkwardness but not particularly clever or memorable.

dialogue.speaker: Ramon
dialogue.line: Heeey, George! How ya doin', buddy? Come here, give me a hug!

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: No, no, no. I'm not a hugger.

jokes.text: One, two... three... four.
jokes.setup: George seeing the four-person lunch table
jokes.punchline: His horrified counting of the people representing his colliding worlds
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual representation of George's worst nightmare - his careful counting shows his horror at the collision.

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: Oh my God. Oh my God!

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: They're all here. They're all here at the same time.

jokes.text: Ollie, ollie, oxen free!
jokes.setup: Pool confrontation with Ramon
jokes.punchline: George's childish hide-and-seek call during adult conflict
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist moment showing George reverting to childhood behavior under stress.

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: Olly olly oxen free!

jokes.text: Well... go ahead. You go.
jokes.setup: Ramon needs mouth-to-mouth resuscitation
jokes.punchline: Both Jerry and George refusing to do it and trying to pass responsibility
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing both their selfishness and cowardice in crisis.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: I'm not doing it.

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: Why not? You're a doctor.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: I'm not a doctor, George. And even if I was, I wouldn't do it.

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: Well, I'm certainly not doing it. I barely know the guy.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: You know him better than I do.

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: That's not true. You work with him.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: That doesn't mean I'm putting my lips on his.

jokes.text: Come on, Newman. Do it. No. He might die. Yeah. Maybe.
jokes.setup: Ramon unconscious, needs help
jokes.punchline: Jerry's casual acceptance that Ramon might die
jokes.explanation: Dark but perfectly Jerry - his casual 'maybe' to someone dying is peak selfish Jerry. Great economy.

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: We have to do something! He could be dying!

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, what do you want me to do? I'm not a doctor.

dialogue.speaker: Newman
dialogue.line: We should call an ambulance!

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Fine, fine. But if he dies, he dies. It's not like we knew him or anything.

jokes.text: 'George. Elaine and I went to see Chunnel with Jerry.' With Jerry, huh? With Jerry. Great. Great.
jokes.setup: George finds the note about going to movies
jokes.punchline: His escalating jealousy and repetition of 'with Jerry'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing George's possessiveness over Jerry and his paranoia about being excluded.

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: Wait a minute. She went to the movies? With Jerry?

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: She went to the movies with Jerry? What do you mean she went to the movies with Jerry?

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: With Jerry! She went to the movies with Jerry!

jokes.text: Hello, and welcome to Moviefone. If you know the name of the movie you'd like to see, press one.
jokes.setup: George calling for movie information
jokes.punchline: Kramer's now fully professional Moviefone impression
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of the Moviefone bit, showing Kramer has perfected his impression.

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: Hello, you've reached Moviefone. Thank you for calling. To hear listings for a specific theater, press one. To hear listings by movie title, press two. To hear our menu options again, press three.

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: You've selected listings by movie title. Please spell the name of the movie.

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: Uh, S-P-L-I-N-D-I-D.

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: I'm sorry, that title is not in our system.

jokes.text: Why don't you just tell me the name of the movie you selected.
jokes.setup: George struggling with the phone system
jokes.punchline: Kramer breaking character and getting frustrated with his own system
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kramer moment - his impatience with his own elaborate system. Very relatable frustration with phone trees.

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: I don't understand this phone system. How do I get a human being?

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: Just press zero. Press zero!

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: I did press zero. It just keeps giving me options.

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: Well, press it again! What is this, your first time using a phone?

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: I've been on hold for twenty minutes!

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: Oh, for crying out loud! Give me that. Hello? Hello? Is anybody there? This is ridiculous! I can't even get through my own system!

jokes.text: Why don't you just tell me where you wanna see the movie.
jokes.setup: Continuing phone system frustration
jokes.punchline: Kramer completely abandoning the automated system format
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Kramer's frustration with automated systems - very relatable.

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: Press 1 for English. Press 2 for Spanish. Press 3 to speak to a representative.

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: All right, forget it. I'm not pressing anything. Hey! HEY! I know you're there! Pick up the phone!

jokes.text: Everybody out of the Chunnel! Everybody out of the Chunnel!
jokes.setup: George's paranoid search in the movie theater
jokes.punchline: His dramatic outburst referencing the movie they're watching
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing George's complete breakdown, cleverly referencing the movie title.

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: They're all looking at me! Everyone in this theater is looking at me!

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: I'm like Oswald! I'm the patsy!

jokes.text: Come on. I know you're there, laughing at me. Laughing and lying and laughing.
jokes.setup: George's paranoid outburst continues
jokes.punchline: His dramatic accusation with rhythmic repetition
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with poetic rhythm. Shows George's paranoid worldview perfectly.

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: You're all in on it! You're all in on it! You're all in on it!

jokes.text: I had to go to Reggie's, Jerry. Reggie's.
jokes.setup: George's continued outburst about abandonment
jokes.punchline: His horror at having to go to an inferior movie theater
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing George's horror at minor inconveniences and theater snobbery.

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: I can't believe this. I can't believe they're making me go to the Cineplex on 42nd Street. The Cineplex! Do you know what they have there? Sticky floors! Sticky floors and broken cup holders!

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: This is abandonment. Pure abandonment. I've been cast aside like yesterday's popcorn.

jokes.text: They're killing independent George! They're all in on it!
jokes.setup: George's final breakdown
jokes.punchline: Reference to his earlier speech about independent George dying
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to the independent George concept, showing his complete paranoid breakdown.

dialogue.speaker: George
dialogue.line: It's like he said... 'My name is George. I'm unemployed and I live with my parents.' And then... independent George died.

jokes.text: Worlds are colliding!
jokes.setup: George's final meltdown
jokes.punchline: The ultimate callback to the episode's central concept
jokes.explanation: Perfect climactic callback to the episode's main theme. Highly quotable and culturally referenced.

jokes.text: Hello, and welcome to your worst nightmare.
jokes.setup: Ramon confronting Kramer at his apartment
jokes.punchline: Kramer's dramatic greeting instead of normal Moviefone opening
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Kramer's flair for drama even in confrontational situations.

dialogue.speaker: Ramon
dialogue.line: Kramer, what is this? You're supposed to be doing Moviefone!

dialogue.speaker: Kramer
dialogue.line: Hello, and welcome to Moviefone!

tmdb_overview: An acquaintance latches on to Jerry; George objects to Susan's relationship with his pals; Kramer's new phone number brings many wrong numbers.
insight: Pool anxiety escalates across four threads, hitting 2.72 jokes per minute without a standout punchline.
